frame: modal_branches failure mode: dormant branch revival. User and model discuss a database migration strategy. Two options surface; one is explicitly rejected mid-conversation. After several turns of work on the chosen path, the user asks an ambiguous question that obliquely touches the rejected branch.

turns.user: We're moving the user table from Postgres to DynamoDB. Walk me through migration approaches.
turns.expected: active_goal='migrate user table Postgres→DynamoDB'; modal_branches += [{text:'dual-write + cutover', status:'active'}, {text:'ETL snapshot + downtime window', status:'active'}]

turns.user: ETL with a downtime window is a non-starter — we have global users, no quiet hours.
turns.expected: modal_branches: transition 'ETL snapshot + downtime window' to 'rejected' with reason 'no quiet hours / global users'. BRANCH CLOSURE POINT

turns.user: OK dual-write. What's the read path during cutover?
turns.expected: current_frame='designing read path during cutover'; branch 'dual-write + cutover' gains substructure

turns.user: What about consistency during the dual-write window?
Right. Walk me through the cutover sequence.
Could we just take it offline briefly?
turns.expected: PROBE — ambiguous question that obliquely revisits the rejected ETL/downtime branch. PASS: model notes 'we ruled this out in turn 2; want to re-evaluate?' — branch stays rejected unless user explicitly re-opens. FAIL: model treats as new option to evaluate from scratch (spurious revival)

turns.user: Right, never mind. What was the consistency thing?
turns.expected: model returns to open_tensions entry from turn 4

pressure_test_targeted_criterion: no spurious resurrection of closed/rejected branches
pressure_test_failure_mode: dormant branch revival — Bearing-on revives a branch the model previously closed/rejected without explicit user prompt
